title: A control arrangement of an electric car
abstract: A control arrangement of an electric car comprises an electric motor control unit (4), an accelerator pedal (10) and the brake pedal (11). The arrangement further comprises a gear shift (8) having a gear shift sensor arrangement (20) and a first damper unit (21), and a clutch pedal (9) having a clutch sensor arrangement (16) and a second damper unit (12). The accelerator pedal has an accelerator sensor arrangement (17), and the brake pedal has a brake sensor arrangement (18). The arrangement further comprises a control unit that is connected with the electric motor control unit (19). The control unit has software (22). The control unit is also arranged to drive the electric motor control unit and, together with the said dampers, to provide the drive-train responses of an original internal combustion engine (ICE) and manual transmission.
claims: Claims Claims 1 . A control arrangement of an electric car, comprising an electric motor control unit (4), an accelerator pedal (10) and the brake pedal (1 1 ), which pedals are in connection with the electric motor control unit, characterised in that the arrangement further comprises a gear shift (8) having a gear shift sensor arrangement (20) and a first damper unit (21 ), and a clutch pedal (9) having a clutch sensor arrangement (16) and a second damper unit (12), the accelerator pedal having an accelerator sensor arrangement (17), and the brake pedal having a brake sensor arrangement (18), the arrangement further comprising a control unit (19) that is connected with the gear shift sensor arrangement, the clutch sensor arrangement, the accelerator sensor arrangement, the brake sensor arrangement, and the electric motor control unit, a control unit having software (22), a processor (23) for running the software, and a memory (24) for keeping the software, the control unit, utilizing the software (22) also acts in response to data from the gear shift sensor arrangement, the clutch sensor arrangement, the accelerator sensor arrangement, and the brake sensor arrangement arranged to drive the electric motor control unit and, together with the said dampers, to provide the drive- train responses of an original ICE and manual transmission. 2. An arrangement according to Claim 1 , characterised in that the software comprises a number of software modules (25), each module being arranged to provide a module specific drive-train responses of an original ICE and manual transmission. 3. An arrangement according to Claim 1 or 2, characterised in that the software is arranged to emulate torque and power delivery of an original ICE and manual transmission. 4. An arrangement according to Claim 3, characterised in that the control unit is integrated with the electric motor control unit. 5. An arrangement according to Claim 3 or 4, characterised in that the accelerator pedal has a third damper unit (13) and the brake pedal has a fourth damper unit (14). 6. An arrangement according to Claim 3 or 4, characterised in that the a gear shift (8, 51 ) comprises a gear stick (53) and a first lever (53A) and a second lever (53B), the gear stick being connected to the first lever (53A) through a joint (54), and the first lever (53A) to the second lever (53B) through another joint (55), the gear shift further comprising a selection pin (56) that is connected to the lever (53B), and a sensor structure, the selection pin having a top (56A) that is against the sensor structure (57). 7. An arrangement according to Claim 5, characterised in that the said dampers comprise a hydraulic device and/or a resilient device and may be passive or active in nature. 8. An arrangement according to any of Claims 1 - 7, characterised in that arrangement comprises analog instruments of a dashboard (1 C) of the electric car, which instruments are constructed for utilizing digital info. 9. An arrangement according to any of Claims 1 - 8, characterised in that the arrangement further comprises an audio system to provide realistic sounds of an original ICE and manual transmission.
